subdomain(s): linear ODE systems and matrix exponentials
polynomial evaluation sums and modular divisibility

problem_spec.summary: Choose discrete random data S (defining a companion-like 3x3 matrix), a nonzero binary initial vector v for X'=A_S X, and a subset T defining a cubic q_T. The event requires a Wronskian-type determinant det[X,X',X''] to be nonzero for all t\geq0 and a separate polynomial-sum invariant Θ_T to be divisible by 6. Compute P(E) exactly and output a three-digit residue derived from the reduced fraction.
givens: S is chosen uniformly among 3-subsets of {1,2,3,4,5,6,7}, written S={s1<s2<s3}.
A_S is the 3x3 real matrix with top two rows [[0,1,0],[0,0,1]] and bottom row [(-1)^{s1}, s2-4, (-1)^{s3}].
T is chosen uniformly among 3-subsets of {0,1,2,3,4,5,6}.
Event E: (i) W(t)\neq0 for all t\geq0, and (ii) 6 | Theta_T.
ask: Compute P(E)=m/n in lowest terms and output the zero-padded three-digit string for ((m+2n) mod 1000).

parameter(s).name: universe_size
parameter(s).purpose: size of the base set for S and T (here 7)

parameter(s).name: matrix_template
parameter(s).purpose: maps S to A_S via parity signs and a centered shift beta=s2-4

parameter(s).name: output_linear_form
parameter(s).purpose: postprocessing uses the linear form m+2n before reducing mod 1000

invariant(s).name: wronskian_reduction_to_static_determinant
invariant(s).statement: For X'=A_S X with X(t)=e^{tA_S}v, we have X^{(k)}(t)=A_S^k X(t), hence W(t)=det[X,AX,A^2X]=det(e^{tA_S}) \cdot det[v,Av,A^2v]=e^{t \cdot tr(A_S)} \cdot Delta_S(v). Therefore W(t)\neq0 for all t\geq0 iff Delta_S(v)\neq0.

invariant(s).name: event_independence_factorization
invariant(s).statement: (S,v) are independent of T, and the W-condition depends only on (S,v) while the Theta-condition depends only on T, so P(E)=P(Delta_S(v)\neq0) \cdot P(6|Theta_T).

invariant(s).name: theta_sum_closed_form
invariant(s).statement: Writing q_T(z)=z^3-s1 z^2+s2 z-s3, we have Theta_T=\sum_{k=0}^4 q_T(k)=100-30s1+10s2-5s3.

invariant(s).name: theta_mod6_structure
invariant(s).statement: From Theta_T=100-30s1+10s2-5s3, the mod-2 condition reduces to s3 even (i.e., T contains an even element). Mod 3, Theta_T \equiv 1+s2+s3, which depends only on the residues of elements of T modulo 3.

essential_assumption(s): The ODE solution exists uniquely for all t\geq0 (true for constant-coefficient linear systems).
Determinant and trace identities over reals apply: det(e^{tA})=e^{t tr(A)}.
Uniform distributions on S, v, and T are independent.
Theta_T is interpreted as an integer; divisibility is in Z.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The finite sample spaces have fixed sizes: |C(7,3)|=35 for S and T, and 7 for v. The W-condition reduces deterministically to Delta_S(v)\neq0, so counting good pairs (S,v) gives a unique rational. Separately, counting T with 6|Theta_T gives a unique rational. Independence fixes P(E) uniquely; lowest-terms reduction fixes (m,n) uniquely and thus the three-digit residue of (m+2n) mod 1000.

technique(s).name: wronskian_to_initial_data_determinant
technique(s).purpose: replace the infinite-time condition W(t)\neq0 for all t\geq0 by the finite check Delta_S(v)\neq0

technique(s).name: discrete_parameterization_of_A_S
technique(s).purpose: encode A_S by (alpha,beta,gamma) with alpha=(-1)^{s1}, beta=s2-4, gamma=(-1)^{s3}

technique(s).name: enumerate_zero_locus_over_binary_vectors
technique(s).purpose: evaluate Delta_S(v) on the 7 nonzero binary vectors and count vanishing cases

technique(s).name: expand_theta_via_symmetric_sums
technique(s).purpose: express Theta_T via symmetric sums and precomputed power sums over k=0..4, then reduce mod 6

technique(s).name: classify_T_by_residues_mod3
technique(s).purpose: count T satisfying the mod-3 component by residue-class patterns, then combine with the parity constraint

technique(s).name: multiply_probabilities_and_postprocess
technique(s).purpose: compute P(E), reduce to m/n, then output zero-padded ((m+2n) mod 1000)

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: reduce an analytic condition to a finite algebraic determinant, then do exact finite counting

critical_trick(s): Use W(t)=e^{t tr(A_S)}\Delta_S(v) to turn "for all t" into an algebraic determinant condition at t=0.
Count (S,v) by classifying A_S via discrete parameters (α,β,γ) and checking \Delta_S(v) on the 7 binary vectors.
Compute Θ_T mod 6 via symmetric-sum expansion and residue-pattern counting modulo 3, with a simple parity exclusion.
Multiply the independent probabilities, reduce to lowest terms, then compute the required three-digit residue of (m+2n) mod 1000.

library(s).name: itertools
library(s).reason: enumerate subsets S,T and vectors v

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: gcd for normalization and modular postprocessing

task(s).name: brute_force_count_Delta_nonzero
task(s).reason: enumerate all 35 subsets S and 7 vectors v, compute Delta_S(v)=det[v,Av,A^2v], count nonzero

task(s).name: brute_force_count_Theta_divisible_by_6
task(s).reason: enumerate all 35 subsets T, compute Theta_T=\sum_{k=0}^4 \prod_{t\inT}(k-t), count those divisible by 6

task(s).name: verify_independence_product
task(s).reason: confirm P(E) equals the product of the two independent proportions
